idea_summary: Initial pipelined kernel: BLOCK_M=128, BLOCK_N=128, BLOCK_K=32, 3 stages
Wide tiles 128x256 with BLOCK_K=32 fits smem, improves N-reuse
K=64 with 3 stages - larger K did not help vs K=32 with 4 stages
64x256 tiles worse than 128x256 - smaller M tiles hurt performance
192x128 tiles worse - taller M does not help
128x128 with 6 stages worse than 128x256 with 4 stages
5 stages worse than 4 stages for this tile configuration
3 stages much worse than 4 stages for 128x256 tiles
128 threads severely limits parallelism - 256 is optimal
256x128 tall tiles worse than 128x256 wide tiles
K=48 triggers TMA fallback warning and bad performance
K=16 with 6 stages worse than K=32 with 4 stages
K=64 with 3 stages worse than K=32 with 4 stages
5 stages with panel_size=9 worse than 4 stages
K=64 with panel_size=6 worse than K=32
5 stages with panel_size=6 worse than 4 stages
3 stages with panel_size=6 worse than 4 stages
panel_size=5 retest from best config - worse
panel_size=7 retest from best config - much worse
64x256 more CTAs but less per-CTA work - worse

128x512 too wide for register file - catastrophic
T.mma does not exist in TileLang - use T.gemm only

K=128 with 2 stages - less latency hiding
